body_txt: Teachers have an important job, educating the youth of a nation. Unfortunately, it's not always an easy job or one that gets respected enough. They could simply be in lighter cases in charge of the Wacky Homeroom and have to deal with all kinds of eccentricities and inane remarks of the kind that would make any Only Sane Man cover their faces in resignation. Worse it could be a Sucky School where the source of their problems lies in the infrastructure. At the worst cases, they will have a group of no-good Delinquents running their classroom. Either way, they're generally Surrounded by Idiots . Whatever the case, these teachers are put upon, worn out, and looking forward to summer vacation almost as much as the kids they teach, possibly more. They may or may not be the Stern Teacher , but odds are they're certainly not the Cool Teacher and even if they were at some point, their experiences probably beat it out of them. Many do try nonetheless not to become a Sadist Teacher , with varying degrees of success. In many instances, these teachers are shown to be caring and concerned for their students, and ofttimes undeserving of some of the trials and tribulations that are their lot in life, making them sympathetic to the audience. In some instances, the teacher does invite the scorn of the students, and so to keep them sympathetic to the audience, they will be the subject of Disproportionate Retribution and/or Comedic Sociopathy . They often have much in common with the Badly Battered Babysitter . Teasing the Substitute Teacher applies when the regular teacher is out sick. If they're bullied too much by their students, they may become an Apathetic Teacher . Common indicators may include Eye Twitch and Cross-Popping Veins . Chain smoking is not unheard of in older works. Overlaps with and possibly a subtrope of Butt-Monkey . Can be Truth in Television , particularly in areas where the prevailing mindset is that teachers are responsible for students' advancement, rather than in cooperation with parents and the students and that there is no parental responsibility which is probably part of the problem.
